interfaces.name: Player
interfaces.signature: struct Player: Codable, Identifiable, Hashable { let id: UUID; var displayName: String; var isHost: Bool }
interfaces.notes: id generated once at name-entry, persisted in UserDefaults, stable across reconnects; displayName never defaults to device name

interfaces.name: ConnectionState
interfaces.signature: enum ConnectionState: Codable, Equatable { case connected; case reconnecting(gracePeriodEndsAt: Date); case lost }
interfaces.notes: drives RosterRow and ResilienceBanner rendering

interfaces.name: RosterEntry
interfaces.signature: struct RosterEntry: Codable, Identifiable { var id: UUID { player.id }; var player: Player; var connectionState: ConnectionState }

interfaces.name: ScenarioGenre
interfaces.signature: enum ScenarioGenre: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case heist, missingPersons, sabotage }
interfaces.notes: maps 1:1 to accent-color design token

interfaces.name: ClueTokenType
interfaces.signature: enum ClueTokenType: String, Codable { case person, location, time, object, relationship }

interfaces.name: FragmentFact
interfaces.signature: struct FragmentFact: Codable, Identifiable, Hashable { let id: String; let tokenType: ClueTokenType; let value: String }
interfaces.notes: atomic unit a player's fragment is built from; TokenComposer may only reference facts the local player owns

interfaces.name: Fragment
interfaces.signature: struct Fragment: Codable, Identifiable { let id: String; let ownerRole: String; let facts: [FragmentFact] }
interfaces.notes: one dealt per player per round

interfaces.name: ClueTokenTemplate
interfaces.signature: struct ClueTokenTemplate: Codable { let type: ClueTokenType; let template: String }
interfaces.notes: constrains TokenComposer's allowed phrasing so a clue can never be freetext

interfaces.name: SolutionKey
interfaces.signature: struct SolutionKey: Codable { let answerText: String; let requiredFactIDs: [String] }
interfaces.notes: used by SolvabilityChecker

interfaces.name: ScenarioPack
interfaces.signature: struct ScenarioPack: Codable, Identifiable { let id: String; let schemaVersion: Int; let title: String; let genre: ScenarioGenre; let premise: String; let centralQuestion: String; let minPlayers: Int; let maxPlayers: Int; let fragmentSets: [Int: [Fragment]]; let solution: SolutionKey; let allowedTokenVocabulary: [ClueTokenTemplate]; let isFree: Bool }
interfaces.notes: fragmentSets keyed by exact player count 6...12; bundled as versioned JSON, loaded by CrosswirePersistence

interfaces.name: Clue
interfaces.signature: struct Clue: Codable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; let authorID: UUID; let authorName: String; let tokens: [FragmentFact]; let renderedText: String; let postedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: board is [Clue] ordered array, ForEach keys off id, never index

interfaces.name: SolutionProposalStatus
interfaces.signature: enum SolutionProposalStatus: Codable { case pending, confirmed, declined }

interfaces.name: SolutionProposal
interfaces.signature: struct SolutionProposal: Codable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; let proposedByID: UUID; let answerText: String; var tally: [UUID: SolutionProposalStatus] }
interfaces.notes: a decline discards this proposal entirely; a new propose creates a fresh id and fresh tally, never reuses

interfaces.name: RoundOutcome
interfaces.signature: enum RoundOutcome: Codable { case solved, unsolved, compromised(missingFragmentID: String) }

interfaces.name: RoundPhase
interfaces.signature: enum RoundPhase: Codable { case briefing, clueExchange, solutionConfirm, resolved(RoundOutcome) }

interfaces.name: RoundSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct RoundSnapshot: Codable { let schemaVersion: Int; let sessionID: UUID; let scenarioPackID: String; var phase: RoundPhase; var roster: [RosterEntry]; var board: [Clue]; let localPlayerID: UUID; let localFragment: Fragment; var remainingSeconds: Int; var activeProposal: SolutionProposal?; var savedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: restore eligibility = phase is non-terminal AND now - savedAt <= graceWindow(60...90s); anything else deleted silently on launch

interfaces.name: RulesEngine
interfaces.signature: protocol RulesEngine { func dealFragments(pack: ScenarioPack, playerCount: Int) -> [Fragment]; func validateClue(tokens: [FragmentFact], against fragment: Fragment) -> Bool; func evaluateSolvability(pack: ScenarioPack, playerCount: Int) -> Bool; func resolveProposal(_ proposal: SolutionProposal, roster: [RosterEntry]) -> SolutionProposalStatus }
interfaces.notes: pure, synchronous, non-isolated; zero imports beyond CrosswireModels; testable without any transport

interfaces.name: PeerID
interfaces.signature: struct PeerID: Hashable, Codable { let rawValue: String }
interfaces.notes: opaque wrapper so nothing above transport touches MCPeerID directly

interfaces.name: GameMessage
interfaces.signature: enum GameMessage: Codable { case roster(RosterEntry); case missionStart(ScenarioPack); case fragmentDeal(Fragment); case clue(Clue); case timerCorrection(remainingSeconds: Int); case solutionProposed(SolutionProposal); case solutionTallyUpdate(SolutionProposal); case roundResolved(RoundOutcome); case hostDisconnect }
interfaces.notes: wire format sent over GameTransport.send/broadcast

interfaces.name: TransportEvent
interfaces.signature: enum TransportEvent { case peerDiscovered(PeerID, name: String); case peerConnected(PeerID); case peerReconnecting(PeerID, gracePeriodEndsAt: Date); case peerLost(PeerID); case messageReceived(GameMessage, from: PeerID); case localNetworkPermissionDenied; case sendFailed(GameMessage, to: PeerID) }
interfaces.notes: emitted only on the main actor; MultipeerGameTransport hops internally before yielding

interfaces.name: GameTransport
interfaces.signature: protocol GameTransport { var events: AsyncStream<TransportEvent> { get }; func startAdvertising(sessionName: String, packID: String); func startBrowsing(); func stopAll(); func send(_ message: GameMessage, to peers: [PeerID]) async throws; func broadcast(_ message: GameMessage) async throws; func disconnect(peer: PeerID) }
interfaces.notes: MultipeerGameTransport and MockGameTransport are the only two conformances; nothing above this protocol imports MultipeerConnectivity

interfaces.name: MockGameTransport
interfaces.signature: final class MockGameTransport: GameTransport { func simulate(peerCount: Int, latencyMs: ClosedRange<Int>, dropRate: Double) }
interfaces.notes: used by RulesEngine and MissionViewModel test suites for deterministic N-peer/latency/drop/reconnect coverage

interfaces.name: RoundSnapshotWriter
interfaces.signature: actor RoundSnapshotWriter { func save(_ snapshot: RoundSnapshot) async; func loadIfRestorable(graceWindowSeconds: Int) async -> RoundSnapshot?; func delete() async }
interfaces.notes: atomic Data.write with .atomic option; coalesces bursty writes, flushes immediately on phase transition and on scenePhase .background

interfaces.name: ScenarioPackLoader
interfaces.signature: struct ScenarioPackLoader { func loadBundled() -> [ScenarioPack] }
interfaces.notes: reads bundled versioned JSON; a build-time test runs evaluateSolvability across player counts 6...12 for every pack

interfaces.name: SettingsStore
interfaces.signature: struct SettingsStore { var displayName: String; var hasSeenRFTip: Bool }
interfaces.notes: thin UserDefaults wrapper

interfaces.name: PurchaseManager
interfaces.signature: @MainActor final class PurchaseManager { func products() async throws -> [Product]; func currentEntitlements() async -> Set<String>; func restore() async throws }
interfaces.notes: StoreKit 2 async APIs; entitlement check is local-only via Transaction.currentEntitlements, no backend

interfaces.name: MissionViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class MissionViewModel { var roster: [RosterEntry]; var board: [Clue]; var localFragment: Fragment; var phase: RoundPhase; var remainingSeconds: Int; var activeProposal: SolutionProposal?; var activeSheet: MissionSheet?; func handle(_ event: TransportEvent) }
interfaces.notes: sole owner of active-round mutable state; every sheet reads this directly, none holds a duplicate copy

interfaces.name: MissionSheet
interfaces.signature: enum MissionSheet: Identifiable { case fragmentTray, composer, solutionConfirm }
interfaces.notes: presented via medium/partial detent so the countdown stays visible

interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Hashable { case nameEntry, home, permissionPrimer, packPicker, lobby(sessionID: UUID), lateJoinBlocked, settings }
interfaces.notes: drives the pre-session NavigationStack path; replaced wholesale (not pushed) by the Mission root once a round starts

interfaces.name: AppRootCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class AppRootCoordinator { var path: [AppRoute]; var activeMission: MissionViewModel?; func restoreIfEligible() async }
interfaces.notes: on launch calls RoundSnapshotWriter.loadIfRestorable before deciding between onboarding and the bounded Restoring-mission transition

interfaces.name: AppLifecycleObserver
interfaces.signature: @MainActor final class AppLifecycleObserver { func sessionDidStart(); func sessionDidEnd(); func scenePhaseChanged(_ phase: ScenePhase) }
interfaces.notes: toggles UIApplication.shared.isIdleTimerDisabled for session duration only; on .background triggers fragment auto-hide and a flush write

interfaces.name: ResilienceBannerSeverity
interfaces.signature: enum ResilienceBannerSeverity: Codable { case info, warning, terminal }
interfaces.notes: severity conveyed in accessibility label text, never color alone

interfaces.name: ResilienceState
interfaces.signature: enum ResilienceState: Equatable { case none; case peerReconnecting(playerName: String, secondsRemaining: Int); case missionCompromised(missingFragmentOwner: String); case hostDisconnected; case restoringMission }
interfaces.notes: drives which ResilienceBanner variant renders on the Mission canvas
